sourceMovie.title: Westworld
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Westworld (1973) as a adventure theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The adventure and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Mad Max: Fury Road
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Westworld clicked for you, Mad Max: Fury Road (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Matrix Revolutions
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Westworld clicked for you, The Matrix Revolutions (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Westworld?
faq.answer: Try Mad Max: Fury Road, The Matrix Revolutions, The Matrix Resurrections—each shares DNA with Westworld while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Westworld?
faq.answer: Westworld (1973) is best described as Adventure, Science Fiction, Western. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Westworld part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Westworld Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
